lessons_eligible_for_lessons_md_in_a_sep: LESSON_S14_D1_001 (eligible; NOT written this turn): RSI(3) bi-directional mean-reversion with a 2N ATR hard stop produced negative in-sample expectancy on a cross-sector cash-equity basket; the hard stop truncates the mean-reversion thesis, converting a >50% win rate into negative expectancy.

LESSON_S14_D1_002 (eligible; NOT written this turn): A cross-sector basket that achieves its diversification target (A7/K10 in band) does NOT rescue a mechanic with a negative per-trade edge; basket construction and signal edge are independent failure axes.

LESSON_S14_D1_003 (eligible; NOT written this turn): FAIL_SAFETY (K1/K2) at S1 baseline IS is fail-closed and upstream of the DR cost-stress chain; P6.5/P10 are moot once the S1 baseline edge is negative. Contrast s13-d1, which was REJECT_FAST of a PROFITABLE strategy by an upstream DR rule.

report_kind: S14-D1-CROSS-SECTOR P7 decision memo: formalizes FAIL_SAFETY verdict driven by K1 (sharpe_proxy/trade<0) at P6 IS S1 baseline (K2 expectancy<=0 co-confirming), UPSTREAM of any DR cost-stress evaluation (P6.5 never reached). Cross-sector diversification thesis HELD (A7/K10 in band); failure is the mechanic edge, not the basket. C6 carried verbatim; no_dr_redefinition_post_seal honored.
title: s14-d1-cross-sector cash-equity P7 decision memo (sealed; binding; candidate-lifecycle TERMINAL by FAIL_SAFETY at P6 IS)

verdict_drivers.name: sharpe_proxy_per_trade_lt_0
verdict_drivers.precedence_note: K1 is the first-firing gate in the IS verdict order (safety counters -> K1 -> K2 -> K4 -> K9). Fires at S1 baseline, UPSTREAM of the DR cost-stress chain; P6.5 was never reached.

verdict_drivers.name: expectancy_le_0
verdict_drivers.note: Co-confirms the negative-edge finding; K1 takes precedence as first-firing.

what_this_outcome_demonstrates: RSI(3) bi-directional mean-reversion with a 2N ATR hard stop has a NEGATIVE in-sample edge on the AAPL/JPM/XOM cross-sector basket at S1 baseline costs: net -$13,754.96 on $100k, expectancy -$39.53/trade, sharpe_proxy/trade -0.1119.
The loss is predominantly GROSS (total costs ~$1.4k of the ~$13.7k loss), so it is a real edge failure, not cost erosion.
A win rate >50% (54.31%) coexisting with negative expectancy is the classic mean-reversion-with-hard-stop signature: many small RSI-exit winners are outweighed by fewer, larger 2N stop-out losers.
FAIL_SAFETY (K1/K2) is a fail-CLOSED outcome at the S1 baseline and is upstream of the DR cost-stress chain; there is no point running P6.5/P10 on a candidate already net-negative at S1.
Per SEAL fail_safety_outcomes_terminal_for_this_candidate_record_id: the candidate lifecycle is TERMINAL.
what_this_outcome_does_NOT_demonstrate: It does NOT demonstrate the cross-sector hypothesis was wrong: A7 (2.09) and K10 (0.4529) sit inside the SEAL's expected bands; the basket diversified as intended.
It does NOT demonstrate a K9/insufficient-sample problem: 348 trades basket-summed (~70/y) clears the 100 floor with ample margin.
It does NOT demonstrate the all-tech sibling DRAFT (214bae0) is non-viable; that is a separate, non-mutually-exclusive candidate and is preserved byte-stable.

It does NOT demonstrate RSI(3) is universally non-viable; under a different exit/stop structure or mechanic family it might differ. THIS sealed spec (RSI(3) 15/55/85/45 + 2N stop) is what failed, and it is locked (no post-SEAL parameter iteration permitted within this candidate).
